text: In this article I evaluate the Keith Ward’s concept of the world view (i.e. world view as understood by humans). According to Ward, in the world view the criterion for assessing the qualities of the world view has been determined by panentheistic ideas. I want to assess the ideas underlying the Reformational evaluation of the panentheism and the bipolar view of God which function as a motivation in various Christian groups. I also try to clarify why these trends in the contemporary philosophy of God are taken by many to be more plausible than classical theism. At the end of this article I give a critical assessment of the intellectual challenge of the generic Christian panentheism, while also taking into account that it can be very tempting to trade the classical doctrine of God for it.
explanation: It uses a formal academic tone with niche terminology, which a human researcher could write. However, the slightly awkward phrasing and repetitive structure are also common in AI-generated prose, so the signal is mixed.
